india_trends.title: India buying Russian oil irrespective of US sanctions waivers, Indian official says
india_trends.summary: India has consistently purchased Russian oil, irrespective of U.S. sanctions waivers, according to a petroleum ministry official. The decision is driven by commercial sense, with ample crude supply secured. This purchasing strategy remains unaffected by any waiver status.

india_trends.title: Markets rebound sharply: Sensex recovers 1,100 points from day’s low, Nifty closes near 23,650
india_trends.summary: Sensex rose 1,100 points from its day’s low to close at 75,315, and Nifty 50 gained 333 points from its day’s low to end the session at 23,650. At close, Sensex was up 77 points and Nifty was up 6 points from Friday’s closing levels.

india_trends.title: Top Gainers & Losers on May 18: Gland Pharma, Coforge, PB Fintech, BSE, Voltas, Bharti Airtel among top gainers
india_trends.summary: Indian stocks marginally gained on May 18, driven by strong tech buying and pharma support, with Nifty 50 up 0.03% at 23,649 and BSE Sensex at 75,263. However, the broader market faced pressure as Nifty Midcap 100 and Nifty Smallcap 100 fell.

india_trends.title: India's cooking fuel shortage is driving up California's gas prices
india_trends.summary: India's cooking gas shortage, driven by the Strait of Hormuz closure, forces refiners to cut alkylate production. This directly impacts California's gasoline supply, as alkylates are crucial for its unique blend, leading to higher prices for motorists already facing a global fuel crisis.

india_trends.title: Mangalam Worldwide’s board approves 1:10 stock split as smallcap rises 120% in a year
india_trends.summary: Mangalam Worldwide has approved a 1:10 stock split, under which each equity share with a face value of Rs 10 will be subdivided into 10 shares of Re 1 each, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals. The decision was cleared by the board on May 15, 2026.

india_trends.summary: The Sensex closed 77 points, or 0.10%, higher at 75,315.04, while the Nifty 50 settled at 23,649.95, up 6 points, or 0.03%.

india_trends.title: India’s earnings growth to slow down in next 12-18 months, warns Moody’s Ratings. Here’s why
india_trends.summary: Moody’s Ratings warns that India’s corporate earnings growth may slow over the next 12–18 months due to rising input costs, rupee depreciation, supply-chain disruptions, and labour market uncertainty. The agency also flagged weaker consumption, delayed investments, and sectoral pressures across auto

india_trends.title: FPIs sell over ₹2 lakh crore in Indian stock market so far in 2026. Are they driving the boom-and-bust cycle?
india_trends.summary: In 2026, FPIs have aggressively sold over ₹2.19 lakh crore in Indian equities, surpassing 2025 outflows. This has weakened the rupee significantly, with total FPI ownership now at 15%. Domestic investors are absorbing some pressure, but concerns over global capital flight persist.
